# Scope and Core Invariants

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/distributionhostinvariantsdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to distribution host invariants](../invariants.md)

This page defines the C11 Distribution / Host Integration boundary. C11 owns host surfaces, distribution-channel restrictions, local helper contracts, external provider CLI provenance, local IPC facts, app data roots, and workspace grants.

C11 does not own provider capability semantics (C3), workspace materialization (C6), runtime process execution (C4), security decision matrices (C7), or SaaS assignment and polling (C10).

## Core Invariants

1. Provider CLIs are never bundled in Riido package artifacts.
2. The Store App is a user-facing control surface for helper state, provider connection state, workspace grants, consent, privacy, telemetry, and review/demo mode.
3. Store App GUI adapters consume C11 contracts; they may own native UI and entitlement calls but cannot bypass C11 facts.
4. Background helper execution is gated by explicit consent and review-aware helper runtime plans.
5. Local IPC is modeled as local-only; platform endpoint details live behind OS adapters.
6. Store channels constrain runtime capability usage even when a provider supports a surface.
7. Review environments must be assessable without a provider CLI present.
8. Public daemon binaries may be release assets, but provider executables remain external user-installed tools.

The executable evidence anchors are `internal/hostintegration`, `tools/storecontract`, and the store distribution contract under `packaging/store`.
